response: In C# it is possible to estimate the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server. This is not exactly like a ping but can be achieved using the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class. You can send a packet and then wait for a response. The time difference between sending and receiving gives an approximate round trip time. However if you only want one direction you cannot measure the time taken for the packet to reach the server because UDP does not guarantee delivery or provide feedback. You can only measure the time taken for the server to respond. To do this you can use the Send and Receive methods of the UdpClient class. You can record the start time before sending the packet and then record the end time when you receive the response. The difference between these two times gives the round trip time. If you do not receive a response you can assume the packet was not delivered. This method does not provide the one way delay but can give an estimate of the total time taken for the packet to travel to the server and back.
